purpose: Add generic platform-level control mechanisms inspired by agentic loop systems such as doom-loop detection, tool routing, approval checks and context management, while keeping Ananta as the control plane. Optionally evaluate ml-intern as a specialized worker/backend.
goal: Ananta should gain stronger generic safeguards and loop-control primitives across CLI, research, evolution and future workers, without coupling the platform to one external agent implementation.
success_criteria: Doom-loop detection works as a generic platform mechanism across task and tool execution paths.
Tool routing becomes a clearer reusable subsystem instead of scattered per-path logic.
Sensitive operations use one consistent approval model across Ananta subsystems.
Context collection, budgeting and compaction are handled by a clearer shared context management layer.
ml-intern can be evaluated or integrated only as an optional specialized worker/backend, not as a competing control plane.

tasks.title: Cross-cutting control layer design define
acceptance_criteria: A design note explains how doom-loop detection, tool routing, approval checks and context management fit into Ananta as platform concerns.
The design states clearly which logic belongs to hub, execution backends and optional specialized workers.
The design avoids creating a second orchestration system inside a worker.

tasks.title: Doom-loop detection target model define
acceptance_criteria: A target model defines what counts as a doom loop across tasks, tools, retries and repeated execution patterns.
The model covers repeated tool calls, repeated failures, no-progress loops and oscillating retry patterns.
The model defines escalating outcomes such as warn, inject correction, require review, pause and abort.

tasks.title: Loop signal collection add
acceptance_criteria: Execution paths emit enough structured signals to detect repeated patterns.
Signals include task id, trace id, tool/backend name, action type, failure type and iteration count.
The collected signals are reusable across CLI, DeerFlow, Evolver and future workers.

tasks.title: Generic doom-loop detector implement
acceptance_criteria: A reusable detector can evaluate repeated execution patterns across runs.
Detection works without being tied to a single backend implementation.
The detector can emit structured outcomes for routing, review or policy handling.

tasks.title: Loop handling policy add
acceptance_criteria: A policy defines how strongly different loop classes are handled.
The policy supports conservative defaults for local and admin-safe operation.
Loop handling is visible in diagnostics and audit trails.

tasks.title: Corrective prompt or corrective context injection define
acceptance_criteria: The system can optionally inject corrective guidance when a loop is detected.
Correction behavior is bounded and configurable.
The mechanism never hides that loop mitigation occurred.

tasks.title: ToolRouter target architecture define
acceptance_criteria: A dedicated tool routing concept is documented for Ananta.
The design explains how routing decisions use task kind, capabilities, policy, governance and backend availability.
The design makes clear that routing stays hub-controlled.

tasks.title: Tool capability catalog normalize
acceptance_criteria: Available tools/backends expose normalized capability metadata.
Capabilities can distinguish research, patching, shell execution, planning, review, admin repair and other classes.
The catalog can be consumed by routing and approval logic.

tasks.title: Reusable tool routing service implement
acceptance_criteria: Tool and backend selection is available through a reusable service rather than scattered conditional logic.
The router can explain why a tool/backend was chosen or rejected.
The router supports fallbacks without bypassing governance.

tasks.title: Routing diagnostics and decision traces expose
acceptance_criteria: Routing decisions can be inspected through existing trace/read-model surfaces.
The output includes chosen tool/backend, alternatives and key reasons.
This remains readable and does not become debug noise for standard users.

tasks.title: Unified approval model define
acceptance_criteria: A single approval model defines low-risk, confirm-required and blocked action classes.
The model applies consistently to shell execution, file mutation, service mutation, install/remove operations, evolver apply and future repair steps.
The approval model is compatible with current governance modes.

tasks.title: Approval check service implement
acceptance_criteria: A reusable approval service can evaluate proposed actions before execution.
The service returns structured reasons and required confirmation level.
Subsystems stop duplicating ad hoc approval checks.

tasks.title: Approval state UX/read-model surface add
acceptance_criteria: Pending approvals, blocked actions and approved actions are visible in relevant views or artifacts.
Users can understand what is waiting for confirmation.
The system remains explicit and auditable.

tasks.title: ContextManager target model define
acceptance_criteria: A shared context management model is documented for Ananta.
The model explains how result memory, task neighborhood, repo retrieval, research artifacts and future wiki knowledge are assembled.
The model defines context budgets, priorities and compaction behavior.

tasks.title: Shared context budget enforcement add
acceptance_criteria: Context assembly enforces budgets instead of blindly appending more material.
Budget logic can reserve space per source or priority class.
Budget use is observable for diagnostics.

tasks.title: Auto-compaction strategy implement
acceptance_criteria: The platform can compact older or lower-priority context into bounded summaries or retrieval documents.
Compaction preserves provenance and does not silently discard critical state.
Compaction is reusable across multi-turn flows.

tasks.title: Context source prioritization rules define
acceptance_criteria: A rule set defines how repo context, task memory, artifacts, research and future wiki sources are prioritized.
The rules reduce context pollution and repeated irrelevance.
The prioritization is explainable in diagnostics.

tasks.title: ContextManager service expose to execution paths
acceptance_criteria: Relevant execution paths consume the same context manager service or contract.
The service is usable from CLI, task propose/execute, research flows and future repair flows.
Existing retrieval functionality is preserved or improved.

tasks.title: Observability for loop routing approval and context add
acceptance_criteria: Metrics or read-model summaries cover loop detections, approval blocks, routing choices and context compaction outcomes.
Operators can see which subsystem is misbehaving.
The observability data remains structured and low-noise.

tasks.title: Contract tests for generic control services add
acceptance_criteria: Tests protect core behavior for doom-loop detection, approval classification, routing output and context compaction.
The services can evolve without silent regression.
The tests do not depend on one specific external worker.

tasks.title: Golden path documentation for safer agentic loops add
acceptance_criteria: There is one documented standard path showing routing, approval, context assembly and loop protection in action.
The example is understandable for reviewers.
The path demonstrates the value of the generic control layer.

tasks.title: ml-intern fit assessment document
acceptance_criteria: A short document evaluates ml-intern as an optional specialized worker/backend for Ananta.
The evaluation distinguishes reusable ideas from direct code reuse.
The document explains why ml-intern should not become a second control plane inside Ananta.

tasks.title: Optional ml-intern worker adapter boundary define
acceptance_criteria: A worker adapter boundary is defined for ml-intern-style specialized execution.
The boundary limits what Ananta delegates and what remains hub-owned.
The adapter model avoids leaking external worker autonomy into platform governance.

tasks.title: ml-intern capability profile model add
acceptance_criteria: If enabled, ml-intern can be described through a capability profile such as ML research, docs, datasets and code support.
The profile is visible to routing and approval logic.
The profile makes it clear that this worker is specialized, not general-purpose default execution.

tasks.title: Optional ml-intern backend spike implement
acceptance_criteria: A minimal spike proves whether ml-intern can be invoked as a bounded external worker/backend.
The spike does not bypass Ananta governance or context rules.
The spike can be discarded cleanly if maintenance cost is too high.

tasks.title: Future specialized worker guidance isolate
acceptance_criteria: The architecture leaves room for future specialized workers beyond ml-intern.
The guidance keeps Ananta generic and worker-agnostic.
This roadmap remains separate from the core control-layer rollout.
